data.content:
This tombstone contains **no .NET runtime frames** to symbolicate. The backtrace consists entirely of:

- **`libc.so`** – Android C library (`strlen`)
- **`libutils.so`** – Android utilities (`String8::set`)
- **`libnativeapp.so`** – App's native JNI library (`Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData`)
- **`libart.so`** – Android ART (Java) runtime

The crash is a `SIGSEGV` (null pointer dereference at `0x0`) originating from a JNI call in a pure native/Java Android app (`com.example.nativeapp`). There are no `.NET` libraries (e.g., `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`) present in the stack trace.
data.reasoningText:
Looking at this tombstone file, I can see the backtrace contains frames from:
- `libc.so` - Android system library
- `libutils.so` - Android system library
- `libnativeapp.so` - The app's native library
- `libart.so` - Android ART runtime

There are **no .NET runtime frames** in this tombstone at all. The app is `com.example.nativeapp` and it's using native code (JNI) through `libnativeapp.so` and the Android ART runtime. There's no `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, `libcoreclr.so`, or any other .NET runtime library in the backtrace.


This means I can't symbolicate any .NET frames since there aren't any to work with.
